description: The oldest continuously operating nine-hole course in Florida, Granada Golf Course opened in January 1923 on a layout designed by Chicago architects William Langford and Theodore Moreau. The par-36 municipal course sits just outside downtown Coral Gables and has remained a beloved neighborhood fixture for over a century.
notes: 9-hole par-36 executive-style municipal course. Oldest operating 9-hole course in FL.

description: Set on the grounds of the landmark Biltmore Hotel, this Donald Ross-designed course opened in 1925 to a celebrity exhibition match featuring Bobby Jones and Gene Sarazen. An extensive restoration by architect Brian Silva in 2007 returned the par-71 layout to its original 1925 routing.
notes: Public municipal course operated by City of Coral Gables. Address: 1210 Anastasia Ave. Restored by Brian Silva in 2007 per original Donald Ross plans.

description: Costa Del Sol Golf Club is an 18-hole public course embedded within the oldest residential community in the City of Doral, designed by Robert E. Cupp in 1974 as one of his earliest solo projects. The par-72 layout stretches 6,011 yards and offers affordable daily-fee play with a driving range and practice facilities on site.
notes: Address: 100 Costa Del Sol Blvd, Doral FL 33178. Distinct from Trump National Doral. Designed by Robert E. Cupp.

description: Designed by P.B. Dye and opened in September 1990, The Links at Fisher Island is a 9-hole layout occupying the interior of a 216-acre private barrier island accessible only by ferry or private yacht. Golf Digest's Ron Whitten has ranked it among the top ten 9-hole courses in the nation.
notes: 9-hole par-35. Ultra-private residential island. Zip 33109 is Fisher Island's own zip. Renovated 2007 by P.B. Dye.

description: One of South Florida's oldest surviving country clubs, Redland Golf & Country Club was designed by Red Lawrence and opened in 1947 amid the agricultural flatlands southwest of Homestead. The par-72 course stretches 6,613 yards and operates on a semi-private basis, welcoming daily-fee players alongside its membership.
notes: Address: 24451 SW 177th Ave, Homestead FL 33031.

description: Crandon Golf at Key Biscayne winds through the subtropical interior of a barrier island just south of Miami, with fairways bending around saltwater lagoons on a course designed by Bruce Devlin and Robert von Hagge in 1972. Ranked among Golf Digest's top 75 upscale public courses, the venue hosted the PGA Tour's Doral Ryder Open satellite events and has undergone a $5 million renovation.
notes: Address: 6700 Crandon Blvd, Key Biscayne FL 33149. Redesigned by Von Hagge 1993. Recent $5M renovation per 2025 reporting.

description: The Hammock Course at Ocean Reef Club was co-designed by Robert von Hagge and Bruce Devlin and opened in 1969, part of a private resort community in North Key Largo whose golf traditions date to a first 9-hole course in 1955. The 18-hole, par-71 layout plays 6,116 yards through a setting of dense tropical foliage accessible only to club members and guests.
notes: Ocean Reef Club is in North Key Largo (zip 33037). Private members-only community.

description: Originally opened as a City of Key West municipal course in 1923 on a Langford & Moreau layout, the Key West Golf Club was comprehensively redesigned by Rees Jones in 1983 into an 18-hole, par-70 course that winds through 200 acres of mangroves and tropical foliage on Stock Island. It is the southernmost public golf course in the continental United States.
notes: Address: 6450 College Rd, Stock Island / Key West FL 33040. Current name is Key West Golf Club. Formerly City of Key West Golf Course. Public.

description: Now operating as Florida Keys Country Club, this Mark Mahannah-designed course opened in 1960 at 4000 Sombrero Blvd in Marathon. Hurricane Irma in 2017 reduced play to nine holes, and the course has continued as a public nine-hole facility in the heart of the Middle Keys.
notes: Now branded Florida Keys Country Club. Reduced to 9 holes after Hurricane Irma (2017). Still open as of late 2025. Address: 4000 Sombrero Blvd, Marathon FL 33050.

description: Originally the Bayshore Golf Course, Miami Beach Golf Club opened in 1923 as part of developer Carl Fisher's Alton Beach subdivision and was fully rebuilt in 2002 by Arthur Hills/Steve Forrest and Associates. The restored 18-hole, par-72 Art Deco-era course is one of the most celebrated public layouts in South Florida.
notes: Address: 2301 Alton Rd, Miami Beach FL 33140. Restored 2002 by Arthur Hills. Public municipal course.

description: La Gorce Country Club was founded in 1927 by Carl Fisher's associate Captain H.C.C. Tippet and has long ranked among the most exclusive private clubs in South Florida. Robert Trent Jones redesigned several holes in 1969, and Jack Nicklaus restored the course in 1993, lending the par-70 layout an architectural pedigree matched by very few clubs of its era.
notes: Address: 5685 Alton Rd, Miami Beach FL 33140. Private, not accepting new membership applicants. Very limited public information.

description: Normandy Shores Golf Club, dedicated on December 18, 1941, is a City of Miami Beach-operated course redesigned by Arthur Hills, with water hazards threading 12 of its 18 holes. The course underwent its most recent renovation in 2008 and remains one of the most affordable public options on Miami Beach.
notes: Address: 2401 Biarritz Dr, Miami Beach FL 33141. City of Miami Beach municipal course.

description: Topgolf Miami Gardens opened December 29, 2017 as the first Topgolf venue in South Florida, offering multi-level climate-controlled hitting bays with gamified ball-tracking technology, a full restaurant, and bar. The 17321 NW 7th Ave facility serves as a sports entertainment destination rather than a traditional golf course.
notes: Entertainment venue, not a traditional course. Opened December 29, 2017.

description: Shula's Golf Club in Miami Lakes was designed by Bill Watts in 1962 and updated in 1998 by Kipp Schulties, producing a 36-hole complex tied to Don Shula's hotel property. The Senator Course offers an 18-hole par-72 championship layout while a separate executive course serves all skill levels, with tee times open to the public.
notes: Address: 7601 Miami Lakes Dr, Miami Lakes FL 33014. Two courses: Senator (18-hole par-72) + Executive (18-hole par-3). Semi-private, public tee times available.

description: Miami Shores Country Club opened December 16, 1939 on a layout by Red Lawrence, partially funded by the federal Works Progress Administration. The 18-hole course operates on a semi-private basis from its Biscayne Boulevard address, offering memberships alongside public daily-fee access.
notes: Address: 10000 Biscayne Blvd, Miami Shores FL 33138. Semi-private.
Skipped: name 'West' in Miami-Dade County is too generic and ambiguous to identify a specific course.
Skipped: name 'East' in Miami-Dade County is too generic and ambiguous to identify a specific course.

description: Key Colony Beach Golf & Tennis is a 9-hole, par-27 public course located at 460 8th St in Key Colony Beach, a small island city in the Middle Keys. The compact layout is maintained by the City of Key Colony Beach and serves as one of the few golf options between Marathon and Key West.
notes: 9-hole par-27 (par-3 course). Address: 460 8th St, Key Colony Beach FL 33051. City-run. Year built ~1968.

description: The Dolphin Course at Ocean Reef Club was designed by Robert von Hagge and Bruce Devlin in 1969, companion to the Hammock Course within this ultra-private North Key Largo resort community. Both courses sit inside a gated enclave accessible only to members and their guests, reached by a private entrance off US-1.
notes: Typo in source name ('Dolphone' should be 'Dolphin'). Ocean Reef Club, North Key Largo. Zip 33037. Private.

description: Greynolds Golf Course is a 9-hole, par-36 public course inside Greynolds Park, designed by Mark Mahannah in 1964 and managed by Miami-Dade County Parks, Recreation and Open Spaces. The tree-lined layout along the Oleta River corridor is one of the county's most accessible and consistently busy public facilities.
notes: Address: 17530 W Dixie Hwy, North Miami Beach FL 33160. Miami-Dade County Parks operated. 9-hole par-36.

description: The Miami Springs Golf & Country Club opened in 1923 as the first golf course built in Miami-Dade County, designed by Thomas 'Tubby' Martin and developed by the Curtiss-Bright Company. The 18-hole, par-71 layout was the site of 30 Miami Open tournaments from 1925 through 1955 and was designated a Miami Springs Historic Site in 2008.
notes: Address: 650 Curtiss Pkwy, Miami Springs FL 33166. Virginia Gardens is a tiny adjacent municipality; course is physically in Miami Springs. Oldest course in Miami-Dade County. Also first in state to allow Black golfers to play.
